\footnotesize
Notes: DSR discoveries are available only at the terminal batch time $T=360$, so DSR delay is defined as $T-A_j$; the protocol is anytime/sequential, and the delay definitions reflect the procedures themselves. SPA (Hansen 2005) and White's Reality Check (2000) are max-over-strategies tests relative to a benchmark, do not provide per-strategy certification, and have no anytime operation here; they are outside this comparison scope and are not run. The DSR comparator is idealized by giving it the exogenous trial count $N=n_{\rm reg}$.
\end{minipage}
